health_zone_table.basis: SitRep58 Table 2 (2026-07-11) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP final PDF (post 25138, media 25141, pdf sha256 c62e6dda952cfacbc159d5735ce2f8e526f66e48d920eb47a632e2835e931185). All five provinces' affected health zones are geolocated and carried as named per-zone rows; the three Tshopo/Kisangani detection communes (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) roll up to the single GRID3 Makiso-Kisangani health zone (4/2), confirmed this cycle. The only residual is the 17 Ituri cases not yet ventilated by health zone.

reconciliation.note: Named health-zone rows sum to 1909 confirmed / 702 confirmed deaths across all five provinces. Table 1 province totals are Ituri 1745/601, Nord-Kivu 173/97, Sud-Kivu 3/1, Haut-Uele 1/1, Tshopo 4/2 = national 1926/702. SitRep 58 folds Haut-Uele and Tshopo into the national total (five provinces); the newly-listed zones are geolocated and carried as named per-zone rows: Ituri Ariwara (1/1), Haut-Uele Wamba (1/1), and Tshopo Makiso-Kisangani (4/2). INSP lists three Tshopo detection communes (Makiso-Kisangani 2/2, Mangobo 1/0, Lubunga 1/0); GRID3 recognises them as the single Makiso-Kisangani (Kisangani) health zone, so their 4 confirmed / 2 deaths are attributed to Makiso-Kisangani, confirmed this cycle (was suspected-watch through SitRep #057). The only unallocated residual is the 17 Ituri confirmed cases in zones not yet identified (0 deaths, the explicit unventilated row); Nord-Kivu Table 1 equals its Table 2 named sum. Sud-Kivu holds 42+ consecutive days with no new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), residual-surveillance.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 227 analyzed / 45 positive (19.9%); Nord-Kivu 123 analyzed / 8 positive (6.5%); Sud-Kivu 6 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 53 positives over 350 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep58 Tableau 7: 753 in isolation at end of day (248 confirmed / 505 suspected, suspected reconciled to the census since the source dont-rows 248+539 overcount vs the 753 census on Nord-Kivu) at 93.9% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu 129.6% saturation, Ituri 87%, Sud-Kivu 66.6%. Exits over 24h (110 total): 15 deaths, 75 non-cases, 12 recoveries, 8 escapes; 94 admissions.
Recovered advanced to 318 DRC cumulative (+12). J-1 bed count is 769; the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 753.
highlights: 53 new confirmed cases and 30 deaths (66.6% community) were reported for 2026-07-11, in Ituri (45 cases) and Nord-Kivu (8 cases). Confirmed cases rose to 1926 and deaths to 702; the global CFR is 36.4%.

SitRep 58 folds Haut-Uele (Wamba) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) into the national total for the first time (previously starred/excluded), and adds Ariwara as a newly affected Ituri health zone; the outbreak now spans five provinces and 42 health zones.

Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 129.6% and global bed occupancy is 93.9% (753 in isolation, 248 confirmed / 505 suspected). 18 UNICEF motorcycles and 10 tonnes of PCI/PECH supplies were received; 25 vehicles and 5 ambulances are available across pillars.

Ituri concentrates the epidemic at 1745 confirmed / 601 deaths, CFR 34.4%. Bunia (507), Rwampara (393), Mongbwalu (336), Nizi (121) and Nyankunde (99) remain the most affected zones; the epidemic is currently progressing fastest in Nizi.
Nord-Kivu totals 173 confirmed / 97 deaths, CFR 56.1%, carried by Katwa (66.2%), Beni (66.7%) and Butembo (47.7%); Katwa (71), Butembo (44) and Beni (30) lead notification.

Sud-Kivu has passed 42+ consecutive days without a new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), holding in the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration; 30 patients remain in isolation.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 3 (11 July): 1170 new alerts + 202 reported (some carried) = 1345 total of the day. Investigated 1179 (87.6%). Validated suspects 228 = 158 living + 70 deaths (surveillance table). NOTE: the cover KPI card shows 299 suspected / 91 deaths, which does not reconcile with this SR58 alert table and appears to be stale carryover from SR57; the internally consistent surveillance value 228/70 is promoted. Per-province validated-death split (Ituri 48 / Nord-Kivu 22 / Sud-Kivu 0) is derived so province rows reconcile to 228 and 158.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 129.6%; national isolation occupancy is 93.9% (753 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 78.3% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert investigation in Ituri (73.9% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers (112 HCW cases, 35 deaths, CFR 31.2%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.3% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two Kisangani (Tshopo) detections (one linked to Nia-Nia) are under investigation and 159912 travelers passed through PoE/PoC this cycle.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu reports a laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis (5.6% positivity) and, in its logistics subset, no functional ambulance or hearse; Mutwanga and Kasindi-Lubiriha flag insufficient IPC inputs and swabs for community-death alerts.
challenges.response: Clear the laboratory backlog, restore transport/EDS logistics, and resupply IPC inputs and swabs to the affected Nord-Kivu health zones.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (11 July): 12735 under follow-up, 9970 seen, 78.3%. Ituri 75.5% (10450/7886), Nord-Kivu 91.2% (2285/2084). Sud-Kivu contact follow-up closed. Tableau 5: 481 new contacts listed (Ituri 364, Nord-Kivu 117), 19 became suspect (Ituri 13, Nord-Kivu 6), 366 completed 21-day follow-up (Ituri 294, Nord-Kivu 72).

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep58 Tableau 7 end-of-day census 753 at 93.9% occupancy (Ituri 535 @87%, Nord-Kivu 188 @129.6%, Sud-Kivu 30 @66.6%). The source dont-rows (248 confirmed + 539 suspected = 787) do NOT sum to the 753 census: Ituri (227+308=535) and Sud-Kivu (0+30=30) reconcile, but Nord-Kivu's split (21 confirmed + 201 suspected = 222) exceeds its 188 end-day row. The census (753) and confirmed (248) are authoritative; suspected-in-isolation is reconciled to the census (753-248=505; Nord-Kivu 188-21=167), correcting the source's inconsistent 201 dont-row. J-1 bed count 769. Exits (110 total): 15 deaths, 75 non-cases, 12 recoveries, 8 escapes; 94 admissions (Ituri 67, NK 21, SK 6).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones, and confirm the SR58 accounting change that folds Haut-Uele (Wamba) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani, Mangobo, Lubunga) into the national total, plus the newly affected Ariwara zone.

Relieve isolation load at 93.9% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 129.6% saturation (56.1% provincial CFR); reconcile the Nord-Kivu confirmed/suspected isolation split and accelerate CTE construction/expansion.
Improve contact follow-up (78.3% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 73.9%, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog / delayed diagnosis.
Sustain residual epidemiological surveillance in Sud-Kivu (42+ days with no new confirmed case) toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration, while resolving the 30 patients still in isolation.

preserved_conflicts: SitRep58 national headline is DRC-only (1926 confirmed / 702 confirmed deaths across five provinces); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.

ACCOUNTING CHANGE vs SitRep 57: SR58 FOLDS Haut-Uele (Wamba 1/1) and Tshopo (Makiso-Kisangani 2/2, Mangobo 1/0, Lubunga 1/0) into the national TOTAL 1926/702 - they are no longer starred; Ariwara (Ituri, 1/1) is newly affected. All five newly-listed zones are geolocated (GeoNames/Wikidata/GRID3) and carried as named per-zone rows this cycle; Makiso-Kisangani moves from suspected-watch to confirmed. Cases are epidemiologically attributed by INSP to the Nia-Nia/PK51 epicentre via population movement, so the map places them as confirmed zones while the corridor/narrative layer carries the imported-origin attribution.

Confirmed-death 24h conflict: the SR58 cover/table give cumulative 702 deaths and 30 deaths in 24h (concordant with the 672->702 cumulative delta), while the page-5 narrative says 29 deaths and cumulative 701. The table/headline value 702/+30 is promoted.

Suspected-count conflict: the SR58 cover KPI card shows 299 suspected cases / 91 suspected deaths, but the surveillance alert table (Tableau 3) shows 228 validated suspected (158 living + 70 deaths). The 299 appears to be stale SR57 carryover; the internally consistent 228/70 is promoted.

Isolation split conflict: the Tableau-7 dont-rows (248 confirmed + 539 suspected = 787) do not sum to the 753 end-day census. Ituri (227+308=535) and Sud-Kivu (0+30=30) reconcile; Nord-Kivu's split (21+201=222) exceeds its 188 end-day row. The 753 census and 248 confirmed are promoted as authoritative and suspected-in-isolation is reconciled to them (753-248=505; Nord-Kivu 188-21=167), correcting the source's inconsistent 201.
Sud-Kivu has passed 42+ consecutive days with no new confirmed case (last case 2026-05-26), in the residual-surveillance phase toward a provincial end-of-outbreak declaration.

review_notes: SitRep #058 (report date 2026-07-11; INSP post 25138; final media 25141; WP publish 2026-07-12T21:47:44Z). PDF sha256 c62e6dda952cfacbc159d5735ce2f8e526f66e48d920eb47a632e2835e931185, 13 pages, 1021161 bytes, byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt (all 13 pages inspected clean).

DRC 1926 confirmed / 702 deaths / CFR 36.4% / 318 recovered; +53 confirmed / +30 deaths in 24h (Ituri 45, Nord-Kivu 8). Isolation census 753 at 93.9% occupancy. Contact follow-up 78.3%. Five provinces / 42 health zones; Ariwara newly affected.

SR58 folds Haut-Uele + Tshopo into the national total (accounting change vs SR57). Country-scope unallocated is 37 (17 Ituri + 20 Uganda). Source-internal conflicts (702 vs 701 deaths, 228 vs 299 suspected, Nord-Kivu isolation split) resolved to table/headline values and preserved in preserved_conflicts.
